verses.text: Moses, a hundred and twenty, says God and Joshua will go over before them. The handoff. The LORD leads, not Moses.
Moses, a hundred and twenty, says God and Joshua will go over before them. The handoff. The LORD leads, not Moses.
Moses, a hundred and twenty, says God and Joshua will go over before them. The handoff. The LORD leads, not Moses.
'Be strong... fear not... he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.' Courage. God's presence steadies them.
'Be strong... fear not... he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.' Courage. God's presence steadies them.
'Be strong... fear not... he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.' Courage. God's presence steadies them.
Moses charges Joshua before all, 'the LORD... will be with thee.' Joshua. The new leader is encouraged.
Moses charges Joshua before all, 'the LORD... will be with thee.' Joshua. The new leader is encouraged.
He writes the law and bids it be read every seventh year. The reading. God's word must be heard.
He writes the law and bids it be read every seventh year. The reading. God's word must be heard.
He writes the law and bids it be read every seventh year. The reading. God's word must be heard.
That all, even children, may hear and learn to fear the LORD. All. Each generation learns.
That all, even children, may hear and learn to fear the LORD. All. Each generation learns.
God calls Moses and Joshua to the tent; the cloud appears. Summoned. God commissions Joshua.
God calls Moses and Joshua to the tent; the cloud appears. Summoned. God commissions Joshua.
God foretells the people will turn to other gods. Foretold. Their apostasy is known.
God foretells the people will turn to other gods. Foretold. Their apostasy is known.
God foretells the people will turn to other gods. Foretold. Their apostasy is known.
A song is given as a witness against them. The song. It will testify in days to come.
A song is given as a witness against them. The song. It will testify in days to come.
A song is given as a witness against them. The song. It will testify in days to come.
Moses writes the song; God charges Joshua to be strong. Charged. The leader is steeled.
Moses writes the song; God charges Joshua to be strong. Charged. The leader is steeled.
The book of the law is put beside the ark as a witness. Witness. The law stands against rebellion.
The book of the law is put beside the ark as a witness. Witness. The law stands against rebellion.
The book of the law is put beside the ark as a witness. Witness. The law stands against rebellion.
The book of the law is put beside the ark as a witness. Witness. The law stands against rebellion.
Moses calls the elders to hear the song. Gathered. He speaks his last words.
Moses calls the elders to hear the song. Gathered. He speaks his last words.
Moses calls the elders to hear the song. Gathered. He speaks his last words.
